(1) The most-complete live loop is in the WRONG binary: engine_runner.rs (the sandbox) already wires live spectral GI @992, dynamic Rapier KCC, particle impact sounds, navmesh agents — while walking_sim.rs (the actual ship target) has none of GI, drop-mechanic, Vello, or CPAL. (2) Every named-missing capability has supporting library code present but UNWIRED: spectral_synth2::strike, cpal_backend, spectral_reverb/sdf_reverb, and the vox_ui vello game-ui feature are referenced by ZERO app binaries. So it is genuinely '~70% built, key pieces sitting on the shelf next to the game rather than plugged in.' (3) walking_sim's 988 lines of game logic have zero tests, so its 'green build' tells you nothing about whether the playthrough works.

punchlist.critical_path:
The dogfood ship target is walking_sim.rs, but every blitz Done-When clause it must satisfy is already implemented and wired ONE BINARY OVER in engine_runner.rs (live spectral GI propagate/apply at engine_runner.rs:990-997, Rapier KCC, particle-impact audio, navmesh agents). So the critical path is NOT "build features" — it is "port/wire the already-live engine_runner features into walking_sim, plus the genuinely-missing library hooks." Ordered, each link blocks ship:

1. (Day 1, FOUNDATION) Decide canonical binary. Either promote walking_sim to consume the engine_runner wiring patterns, or designate engine_runner the ship target. Every downstream wiring task depends on this. Recommend: keep walking_sim as dogfood, port the 4 missing wirings into it. CI green-gate web/spectra continue-on-error.
2. (Day 1) Wire SpectralRadianceCache.propagate/apply into walking_sim's render path (the only missing live-loop GI; engine_runner already proves the pattern at :990-997). This is the foundation other domains read from (audio reverb, UI HUD, AI perception all want live band energy). BLOCKS: spectral-HUD, reverb-from-GI, perception-decision.
3. (Day 2) Add a dynamic Rapier body + drop-key + spectral_synth2::strike (no-WAV) impact in walking_sim. The drop-object-that-falls-and-sounds mechanic is the single most-cited missing Done-When clause and is referenced by physics+audio+integration. add_dynamic_box does not exist in any live loop today.
4. (Day 2) Fix script I/O hollowness: populate SpectralState.band_energy from the live GI cache (engine_runner.rs:441/2074) and EntityStore from ECS — without this every script path returns zeros and can never fire a threshold. Depends on (2).
5. (Day 3) Fix the VXM V3-write / V1-read mismatch (vox_tools writes version=3, import_pipeline/engine_runner read only version=1) and add a .vxm load path to walking_sim. Without this the entire asset pipeline produces files the game cannot open. Verified live.
6. (Day 5) Vello+Taffy SpectralHUD convergence: enable the game-ui feature (currently enabled by NO crate), add text via parley, route walking_sim HUD through VelloCtx reading the live GI cache from (2). Multi-day; the biggest single risk.
7. (Day 7) Integration dogfood: one continuous walking_sim playthrough hitting walk+collect+drop+sound+GI+HUD, plus the FIRST automated test of walking_sim's 988 LOC (currently zero).

If Day-6 Vello slips, fall back to the bitmap HUD reading live GI (keeps the "non-constant radiance into UI" clause via vox_core::game_ui) and ship without Vello — do not let Vello block the playthrough.
punchlist.headline: REALITY: this is ~50% done-and-playable, not 80-95%. Of 18 audited domains, only 2 are genuinely wired-and-functional end-to-end (terrain SDF->splat/navmesh, and the core Rapier static-collider step). The build is green with 2059 tests, but the tests are the trap: they prove library logic in isolation, not live wiring. Wired-status tally: 0 fully-wired-AND-functional headline domains; 11 "partially" (live hook exists but the data flowing through it is inert — empty maps, zeroed band-energy, discarded results, None Options); 5 effectively NOT wired (net, ui-vello, web, plus the GameScript trait and most of audio/AI). The single structural root cause: the dogfood ship target (walking_sim.rs) is the WRONG binary — the richest live loop (spectral GI, KCC physics, impact audio, navmesh AI) lives in engine_runner.rs ("ochroma" bin), while walking_sim has none of GI/drop-mechanic/Vello/CPAL. Every named-missing capability has real library code sitting UNWIRED next to the game: spectral_synth2::strike, cpal_backend, VelloCtx, blend_gpu (no setter exists), GpuGiPass (never dispatched), dynamic Rapier bodies (never spawned), QUIC (connect() discards the handshake future). Verified live: walking_sim has 0 refs to vox_sim/GI/Vello/dynamic-body; engine_runner has 0 GameScript wiring; quic_transport and character_body are both permanently None; game-ui cargo feature is enabled by no crate; VXM writer emits v3 while the loader rejects everything but v1.
punchlist.surprises:
1. The headline scripting framework in vox_core (GameScript/ScriptRegistry/ScriptComponent) is DEAD — zero callsites in vox_app (verified). Worse, script_update_system (engine_runtime.rs:234) re-creates each script instance from scratch every frame, so it is structurally incapable of stateful scripts even if wired. The live game scripts via Rhai+Lua instead, but THOSE read inert zeroed stores too. Three script systems coexist despite the plan saying "replace Rhai with mlua."

2. The most-complete live loop is in the WRONG binary. engine_runner.rs (the sandbox) wires live spectral GI at :990-997, dynamic KCC, particle impact sounds, navmesh agents. walking_sim.rs (the actual ship/dogfood target) has NONE of them. The blitz can largely be won by porting, not building.

3. "GPU" is frequently a misnomer. gpu_particles::GpuParticleSystem is a CPU Vec with no wgpu pipeline. GpuGiPass has a real shader but is never dispatched. PBF GPU path exists but only cpu_step is reachable. blend_gpu has NO setter anywhere — the GPU blend path is structurally unreachable, not merely unwired.

4. Doc drift in the governing roadmap itself: it asserts an 8-band [u16;8] cross-system invariant, but GaussianSplat is actually [u16;16] and the GI code correctly uses 16. The roadmap is wrong; the code is consistent. Multiple named acceptance tests (gi_cache_is_live, blend_tree_interpolates, gltf2splat_cube, gizmo_drag_updates_translation) DO NOT EXIST. Several Done-When CLI invocations (--llm-scene, gltf2splat subcommand, cargo run -- out.vxm) are unrunnable today.

5. Two false-green CI traps: web/index.html unconditionally shows "Ready" over a blank black canvas with no WebGPU init, and the CI build-web job lacks continue-on-error so an empty-wasm compile passes green and signals "web works" when nothing renders. No audio assets exist on disk, so even the wired rodio file-playback paths only produce decode errors. The single live spectral-audio path actively VIOLATES its own spec (writes /tmp WAV when spec demands "no WAV file").

6. VXM round-trip is broken across the seam: each path is green in isolation (v3 writer test passes, v1 reader test passes) but they are incompatible versions, and NO test exercises CLI-write -> game-read. Classic "green tests, broken integration."
punchlist.cross_domain_risks:
1. THE BINARY-SPLIT RISK (highest). Three independent ApplicationHandlers (walking_sim dogfood, main.rs city-builder, engine_runner sandbox) with no shared game loop. Any task that says "wire X into the live game" is ambiguous until the canonical binary is fixed on Day 1. If two agents wire the same feature into two different binaries, collision + wasted work. MITIGATION: Day-1 decision, written into every task as "target = walking_sim.rs".

2. THE LIVE GI CACHE IS A SHARED DEPENDENCY HUB. Spectral-HUD (ui), reverb-from-GI (audio), script SpectralState (script), and AI perception-decision (ai) ALL want to read live per-band radiance. Today that cache is only live in engine_runner; in walking_sim it does not exist. If GI-into-walking_sim (Day 1) slips, four downstream domains cannot satisfy their Done-When. This is the most fan-out-heavy single task. Sequence it first and lock its public read API (cache.band_energy_at(pos) -> [f32;16]) before the four consumers start.

3. SHARED-FILE COLLISION IN walking_sim.rs AND engine_runner.rs. Physics-drop, audio-impact, script-hook, GI-wire, and Vello-HUD ALL edit walking_sim.rs's update()/render(). These cannot be parallelized against the same file without merge hell. Either serialize all walking_sim edits behind one owner per day, or stage them as separate well-bounded functions. This is why parallel_safe_now lists only NON-walking_sim crate-internal work.

4. VELLO FEATURE-FLAG BLAST RADIUS. Enabling game-ui pulls vello/parley/taffy into the build for the first time; it may break the GREEN build (new deps, wasm interaction, surface/format/depth ordering vs the egui pass — all unverified per audit). Quarantine on a branch; do not let it touch the Day-1/2/3 critical path. Have the bitmap-HUD-reading-live-GI fallback ready.

5. VXM VERSION MISMATCH is a cross-crate seam (vox_tools writes, vox_data reads, vox_app loads). Fixing it touches three crates; coordinate so the version constant and the round-trip test land together.

6. TRACK S OVERLAP: vox_render is shared between blitz (CPU/crucible GI, Vello) and Track S (Vulkan/Slang). Any blitz vox_render edit risks colliding with spectra-native bring-up. Confine blitz vox_render work to spectral_gi.rs/spectral_atmosphere.rs/animation_driver.rs (CPU paths) and avoid the GPU backend/pipeline files Track S owns.

critic:
Fully confirmed and worse than the punch-list stated. The seam is concretely:
- `ochroma-tools` (the shipping CLI, `ochroma_main.rs:65`) writes **`VxmFileV3`** (v3).
- `import_pipeline.rs:198` and `engine_runner.rs:1825` both read with **`VxmFile::read`** which rejects anything but v1 (`vxm.rs:124`).
- So the flagship tool's output is unreadable by both the import pipeline AND the live game. The punch-list said "vox_tools writes v3" — it's actually the `ochroma-tools` binary specifically, and `vox_tools/main.rs` writes v1 (loadable). The fix must reconcile which CLI is canonical.

I have enough verified evidence across all gap categories. Final synthesis.

## Coverage gaps the audit missed

**(1) Whole crates / modules never audited**

- **`vox_sim` (6,260 LOC) is a second, fully-wired game.** It's the city-builder sim (citizens, zoning, traffic, economy, roads, services, trade, employment, weather) and it IS live-wired into `vox_app/src/simulation.rs`, `main.rs`, `growth.rs`, `placement.rs`, `road_builder.rs`. The punch-list's "binary-split risk" names three binaries but treats `main.rs` as just "city-builder"; in reality `main.rs` drives an entire 6.2k-LOC simulation that no domain audit examined. If walking_sim is the dogfood target, this entire subsystem is orphaned ship-weight that still must compile and stay green.
- **`ochroma_engine` is the advertised public SDK and is depended on by NOTHING.** It re-exports every crate as `ochroma_engine::{core,render,sim,...}` and ships a `prelude`, with `tests/sdk_test.rs`. The `prelude` (lib.rs:78-105) is a hard-coded list of paths — any of those that have moved/renamed will fail only when someone uses the SDK, which CI's `--no-default-features` test of `ochroma_engine` may or may not exercise. This is the public face of the engine and it's untested against real consumer use.
- **`vox_nodes` (427 LOC, `OchrGraph`/`mat_nodes`) was not in the audit list** yet is a live dep of `vox_render` (material_editor_ui, anim_editor_ui, vfx_editor_ui). The editor node-graph the Day-5 task wants to make functional partly lives HERE, not in vox_editor.
- **`vox_nn` was folded into "ai" but is a separate crate** with its own `llm.rs` save/load and is depended on by vox_app AND ochroma_engine. The `--llm-scene` Day-6 task touches a crate the AI audit may not have opened.
- **Three more binaries + three example projects went unexamined:** `platformer.rs` (667 LOC), `demo.rs` (847 LOC, imports DLSS/temporal/mega-geometry), `render_showcase.rs` (671 LOC), `hello_splat.rs`, plus `examples/{demo_scene,simple_game,city_builder}`. `platformer.rs` already has a working spectral-framebuffer + tonemapper render path (not GI, but more than walking_sim's render) — it may be a better port-source than the audit's "engine_runner only" framing. The `examples/` projects are **NOT workspace members** (no Cargo membership), so they are never built by anything and will silently rot.

**(2) Cross-cutting concerns no single domain owns**

- **CI is a workspace-wide false-green, not just web.** `ci.yml` runs ONLY `cargo clippy/test --workspace --no-default-features`. But `vox_render` default = `["crucible"]` and `vox_audio` default = `["audio-backend"]` (rodio/cpal/fundsp/lewton). **CI never compiles the crucible GI renderer and never compiles the entire CPAL/rodio audio backend** — exactly the two subsystems the blitz wants to wire. The 2,061 tests (verified count) prove the no-backend paths only. `RUSTFLAGS:-D warnings` applies only to the no-default path; the default build emits warnings (verified) that would never gate. **No CI job builds ANY windowed binary** (walking_sim/engine_runner/platformer/demo) — they can break and CI stays green. This is the highest-leverage miss in the whole audit.
- **Save/load is a real subsystem the audit skipped:** `vox_data/world_save.rs`, `vox_app/persistence.rs`, `vox_data/prefab.rs`, wired into `main.rs` and `engine_runner.rs` but not walking_sim. If walking_sim is the ship target, it has no persistence and the existing save/load code is exercised only by the abandoned binary.
- **Hot-reload is a 5-module subsystem (`vox_data/hot_reload.rs`, `vox_core/asset_watcher.rs`, `vox_core/hot_reload_system.rs`, `vox_render/material_hotreload.rs`, `vox_script` reload) that is wired into NO live game** — `render_showcase.rs` instantiates `AssetWatcher` for a one-shot print, and engine_runner has it commented out (lines 63-64). Pure dead weight nobody audited.
- **ECS schedule/ordering has no owner.** System registration is scattered across `vox_render/{render,particle,lod}_ecs.rs`, `vox_physics/ecs.rs`, `vox_audio/ecs.rs`, `vox_app/{soundscape,ai_fsm}.rs`, `main.rs`. No domain audited whether these compose into one coherent `Schedule` with correct before/after ordering, or whether walking_sim even uses bevy_ecs at all (it appears to be a hand-rolled `ApplicationHandler`, meaning the entire ECS schedule investment may be bypassed by the ship target).
- **Cross-platform (Win/Mac) is asserted by `cargo-dist` targets + CI matrix but unverifiable:** the CI matrix runs `windows-latest`/`macos-latest` but only on the no-backend build, so the cpal (platform-specific audio) and crucible paths are never built on any non-Linux runner. `release.yml`/cargo-dist will be the first thing to ever compile the real binary on Win/Mac — at release time.

**(3) "Wired" claims that still smell unverified**

- The VXM mismatch is concretely worse than stated: there are **two tool binaries** — `ochroma-tools` (`ochroma_main.rs:65`) writes `VxmFileV3` (v3), while `vox_tools` (`main.rs:78`) and `turnaround.rs` write `VxmFile` (v1). The readers (`import_pipeline.rs:198`, `engine_runner.rs:1825`) call `VxmFile::read`, which hard-rejects non-v1 (`vxm.rs:124`). So the *flagship* CLI's output is unloadable, but the *legacy* CLI's is fine. The fix must first decide which CLI is canonical — the punch-list treats it as one writer.

**(4) Integration seams no single audit checked**

- **vox_sim ↔ vox_app simulation loop** (does the 6.2k-LOC sim actually tick under any running binary, or only in unit tests?).
- **ochroma_engine prelude ↔ moved APIs** (SDK re-export drift).
- **examples/ ↔ workspace** (orphaned, unbuilt — will not compile against current APIs).
- **release.yml/cargo-dist ↔ default-feature build** (first real cross-platform compile happens at release, untested in CI).

## Single most important thing to verify next

**Run `cargo build --workspace --all-targets` (default features) and a per-binary `cargo build --bin {walking_sim,engine_runner,platformer,demo}` on a clean tree, then add those exact commands to CI.** Everything in this plan ("wire X into the live game," "port engine_runner features," "make audio reachable") presumes the default-feature build and the windowed binaries compile — but the ONLY build CI runs is `--no-default-features`, which excludes the crucible GI renderer and the entire CPAL audio backend the blitz depends on. Until you prove the default build of the actual ship binary is green, every Day-1-to-7 wiring task is building on unverified ground, and the "2,061 green tests / green build" headline is measuring a configuration that contains none of the features being shipped.
